title: One Subnet Unreachable Behind a Healthy-Looking Route Summary

ticket.summary: Monitoring for the finance app server at 10.30.14.22 (subnet 10.30.14.0/24) has been alerting 'host unreachable' since yesterday afternoon, but every other subnet in the 10.30.0.0/16 range (used by regional distribution router dist-rtr03) is working normally. Traceroutes from the core toward 10.30.14.22 die at dist-rtr03 with no further hops.

topology.description: dist-rtr03 aggregates eight /24 subnets (10.30.8.0/24 through 10.30.15.0/24) for the west campus and advertises them into the OSPF backbone as a single summarized route, 10.30.8.0/21, to keep the core routing table small. Each /24 is normally connected to one of dist-rtr03's downstream access switches. Two weeks ago, subnet 10.30.14.0/24 (finance servers) was physically re-homed to a different distribution router, dist-rtr07, as part of a rack consolidation, but the summarization on dist-rtr03 was never updated, so dist-rtr03 still advertises 10.30.14.0/24 as part of its summary even though it no longer has a real path to it.

links.label: OSPF area 0, no route advertised for 10.30.14.0/24 yet

logs.content:
Jul 13 14:20:11 core-rtr01 %ICMP-3-UNREACH: destination unreachable, host 10.30.14.22 via 10.30.8.253 (dist-rtr03)
Jul 13 14:20:11 core-rtr01 route-lookup: 10.30.14.22 matched summary 10.30.8.0/21 via dist-rtr03 (most specific match available in backbone table)

Jul 13 14:20:11 dist-rtr03 %IP-3-NOROUTE: no route to host 10.30.14.22, discarding
Jun 30 09:44:02 dist-rtr03 %LINK-3-UPDOWN: Interface Vlan14 (10.30.14.0/24), changed state to administratively down (rack consolidation - moved to dist-rtr07 per CHG0041882)

router ospf 1
 area 0 range 10.30.8.0 255.255.248.0

 network 10.30.11.0 0.0.0.255 area 0

 network 10.30.13.0 0.0.0.255 area 0
 network 10.30.15.0 0.0.0.255 area 0
! note: 10.30.14.0/24 no longer advertised here directly, but area range summary below still covers it

Routing entry for 10.30.8.0/21
  Known via "ospf 1", distance 110, metric 20, type inter area
  Last update from 10.30.8.253 on Gi0/1, 00:14:22 ago
  Routing Descriptor Blocks:
  * 10.30.8.253, from 10.30.8.253, via GigabitEthernet0/1

C    10.30.14.0/24 is directly connected, Vlan14
     (locally connected, not yet advertised into OSPF area 0 - network statement missing)

monitoring.notes: Synthetic reachability checks show 10.30.14.0/24 (finance) dropped to 0% reachability at 2026-06-30 09:44, exactly when the Vlan14 interface on dist-rtr03 was administratively shut down for the rack move — but the alert was suppressed at the time as 'planned maintenance' and never re-verified after the move completed. All seven other /24s under the 10.30.8.0/21 summary have shown 100% reachability the entire time, which is why this went unnoticed for two weeks: the aggregate summary route looked completely healthy.

hints: Every other subnet under dist-rtr03's summary works fine — the problem is specific to one /24, which suggests the issue is inside the summary boundary, not with OSPF adjacency or the backbone itself.
Check whether dist-rtr03 still has a real, directly connected route for 10.30.14.0/24, separate from the area range summary it advertises.

dist-rtr03's area 0 range 10.30.8.0/21 summary still includes 10.30.14.0/24, but Vlan14 was shut down there on 2026-06-30 when the subnet moved to dist-rtr07 — and dist-rtr07 has the subnet connected locally but never added it to an OSPF network statement, so no router in the backbone has a real path to it.

solution.root_cause: Subnet 10.30.14.0/24 was physically moved from dist-rtr03 to dist-rtr07 during a rack consolidation, but dist-rtr03's OSPF area 0 range summary (10.30.8.0/21) still covers that subnet even though it no longer has a real route to it, and dist-rtr07 never added 10.30.14.0/24 to an OSPF network statement to advertise it. The result is a routing black hole: the backbone still matches 10.30.14.22 to the summary via dist-rtr03, which silently discards the traffic.
solution.fix: Add the 10.30.14.0/24 network statement to OSPF on dist-rtr07 so it's actually advertised into area 0, and verify dist-rtr03's area range no longer misleadingly summarizes a subnet it doesn't own (or narrow the range if other subnets still legitimately belong to it).
